english: Good morning! What can I get for you?
choicesEnglish: Good morning! What can I get for you?
Hi! Where are you going?
Hello! How much is everything?
Good evening! Did you book?
correctAnswerEnglish: Good morning! What can I get for you?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Il saluto tipico del panettiere al cliente.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la domanda classica per prendere l'ordinazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The typical baker's greeting to the customer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the classic question for taking an order.

english: I'd like a piece of focaccia.
choicesEnglish: I'd like a piece of focaccia.
I'd like a bag of mud.
I'd like a trip tour.
I'd like a king's kiss.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'd like a piece of focaccia.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una richiesta comune in panetteria.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo una porzione di focaccia.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A common request at the bakery.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for a portion of focaccia.

english: Is it fresh from today?
choicesEnglish: Is it fresh from today?
Is it expensive from yesterday?
Is it long from now?
Is it fake from later?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is it fresh from today?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere conferma sulla freschezza del prodotto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se il pane è stato fatto stamattina.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for confirmation on the product's freshness.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if the bread was made this morning.

italian: È uscito dal forno mezz'ora fa.
english: It came out of the oven half an hour ago.
choicesItalian: È uscito dal forno mezz'ora fa.
È perso dalla via un'ora fa.
È riso dalla festa un giorno fa.
È gridato dalla news un mese fa.
choicesEnglish: It came out of the oven half an hour ago.
It was lost from the street an hour ago.
It was laughed at the festival a day ago.
It was shouted from the news a month ago.
correctAnswerItalian: È uscito dal forno mezz'ora fa.
correctAnswerEnglish: It came out of the oven half an hour ago.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una garanzia di assoluta freschezza.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che il prodotto è ancora tiepido.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A guarantee of absolute freshness.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the product is still lukewarm.

choicesEnglish: How much (of it) do you want?
Who calls for it?
Where does it run?
When is it silent?
correctAnswerEnglish: How much (of it) do you want?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La domanda del panettiere sulla quantità.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alla misura del peso richiesta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The baker's question about the quantity.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the weight measure requested.

choicesEnglish: 100 grams, please.
A kilogram, thanks.
A liter, you're welcome.
A meter, right away.
correctAnswerEnglish: 100 grams, please.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una porzione piccola, ideale per un assaggio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo cento grammi di prodotto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A small portion, ideal for a taste.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for a hundred grams of product.

choicesEnglish: I'll take half a kilo.

I see half a meter.
I shout half an etto.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'll take half a kilo.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai specificando il peso totale dell'acquisto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A medium quantity, about 500 grams.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are specifying the total weight of the purchase.

choicesEnglish: How much focaccia is left?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Esprimere una preferenza alimentare precisa.

correctAnswerEnglish: The whole-wheat one was good.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un giudizio positivo su un acquisto precedente.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai lodando la versione scura del pane.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A positive judgment on a previous purchase.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are praising the dark version of the bread.

italian: La do in omaggio per il disagio.
english: I'm giving it as a gift for the inconvenience.
choicesItalian: La do in omaggio per il disagio.
La perdo in via per il regalo.
La chiamo in bar per il debito.
La vendo in cassa per il bacio.
choicesEnglish: I'm giving it as a gift for the inconvenience.
I'm losing it on the street for the gift.
I'm calling it in the bar for the debt.
I'm selling it at the register for the kiss.
correctAnswerItalian: La do in omaggio per il disagio.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'm giving it for free for the inconvenience.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un omaggio del panettiere per scusarsi della pagnotta dura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un gesto riparatore gratuito verso il cliente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A gift from the baker to apologize for the hard loaf.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a compensatory free gesture toward the customer.

italian: Io vorrei un po' di tutto.
english: I'd like a bit of everything.
choicesItalian: Io vorrei un po' di tutto.
Io odio un po' di news.
Io temo un po' di tour.
Io scelgo un po' di bar.
choicesEnglish: I'd like a bit of everything.
I hate a bit of news.
I fear a bit of tour.
I choose a bit of bar.
correctAnswerItalian: Io vorrei un po' di tutto.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'd like a bit of everything.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Desiderare una varietà di prodotti diversi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi assaggiare più cose tra quelle esposte.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Desiring a variety of different products.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to taste more things among those on display.

english: The dark multi-grain one.
choicesEnglish: The dark multi-grain one.
The light mud one.
The red news one.
The fake kiss one.
correctAnswerEnglish: The dark multi-grain one.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Scegliere la versione con semi e farina integrale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi la focaccia o il pane fatto con cereali vari.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Choosing the version with seeds and whole-wheat flour.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate the focaccia or bread made with various grains.

italian: Me lo taglia per i toast?
english: Can you slice it for toast?
choicesItalian: Me lo taglia per i toast?
Me lo rida per i baci?
Me lo chiama per i re?
Me lo perde per i lupi?
choicesEnglish: Can you slice it for toast?
Can you give it back for the kisses?
Can you call him for the kings?
Can you lose it for the wolves?
correctAnswerItalian: Me lo taglia per i toast?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you slice it for toast?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una richiesta specifica per il formato delle fette.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi il pane pronto da inserire nel tostapane.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A specific request for the slices' format.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want the bread ready to be put in the toaster.

english: Only four are left.
choicesEnglish: Only four are left.
Only three are lost.
Only two are seen.
Only one is shouted.
correctAnswerEnglish: Only four are left.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Informare sulla scarsa disponibilità rimanente.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il panettiere ti dice quanti pezzi restano.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Informing on the scant remaining availability.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the baker tells you how many pieces are left.

english: Goodbye.
choicesEnglish: Goodbye.
Good morning.
Good evening.
Hi dear.
correctAnswerEnglish: Goodbye.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Il saluto standard quando si lascia un negozio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la formula di cortesia finale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The standard greeting when leaving a shop.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it is the final polite formula.

english: Enjoy your meal.
choicesEnglish: Enjoy your meal.
Have a good trip.
Good night.
Have a good workday.
correctAnswerEnglish: Enjoy your meal.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! L'augurio ideale per chi ha appena comprato del pane.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai augurando a qualcuno di gustarsi il cibo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The ideal wish for someone who has just bought bread.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are wishing someone to enjoy their food.
